name: Cash America Pawn
description_short: FirstCash is the leading international operator of pawn stores with 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states, the UK, and Latin America. They offer pawn loans, gold buying, retail sales, and layaway services.
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc. is headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas and operates as the leading international pawn store operator with over 3,300 retail locations across 29 U.S. states, Washington D.C., the United Kingdom, and Latin America (including all Mexican states plus Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador). The company employs approximately 22,000 people and is included in both the Standard & Poor's MidCap 400 Index and the Russell 2000 Index, indicating it is a publicly traded mid-cap corporation.

FirstCash's primary service is pawn lending, offering small non-recourse loans secured by pledged personal property. Customers can pawn jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, musical instruments, and other merchandise for immediate cash. Beyond pawn loans, FirstCash buys gold, silver, and platinum on the spot for cash. The company also operates retail sales of used merchandise, a layaway service (requiring 10% down payment), and gold-buying services. Through its subsidiary AFF, FirstCash additionally provides lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions through over 15,000 retail merchant partner locations nationwide.

FirstCash distinguishes itself through its massive scale—3,300+ locations make it the largest pawn operator internationally—and its dual business model combining traditional pawn lending with technology-driven point-of-sale payment solutions via AFF. The company explicitly targets cash and credit-constrained consumers who may lack access to traditional lending. Their layaway service with only 10% down provides an alternative to credit-based purchasing.

As a pawn lender, FirstCash serves consumers needing immediate liquidity who have tangible collateral. However, pawn loans typically carry high effective interest rates due to short terms, and customers risk losing pledged items if unable to repay. The company's scale and public market status may mean less negotiation flexibility compared to independent pawn shops. Consumers should understand that pawn loans are non-recourse (collateral-based only) but still carry real financial costs.
pros: Massive network of 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states, UK, and Latin America for accessibility
Immediate cash on the spot for pawn loans and gold sales—no waiting or credit check required
Non-recourse pawn loans mean no additional debt collection or credit reporting if you don't repay
Buys wide variety of items (jewelry, electronics, tools, instruments, sporting goods, appliances)
Layaway service available with only 10% down payment as alternative to credit cards
Subsidiary AFF network provides lease-to-own and retail financing through 15,000+ partner locations
Publicly traded company (S&P MidCap 400, Russell 2000) suggests financial stability and transparency
cons: Pawn loans carry high effective interest rates due to short loan terms (typically 30-90 days)
Risk of losing pledged collateral if unable to repay loan within specified timeframe
As a large corporation, less flexibility for negotiation compared to independent pawn shops
Layaway service requires 10% down and extended payment plan, tying up cash upfront
Primary service targets cash-constrained consumers, implying this is not a low-cost solution
best_for: Consumers needing immediate cash who own valuable collateral (jewelry, electronics, instruments)
People with poor credit or no credit history unable to qualify for traditional personal loans
Individuals seeking short-term liquidity without the risk of credit reporting or debt collection
Those wanting to sell gold, silver, or platinum quickly for cash at any of 3,300+ locations
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for consumers with valuable collateral who need immediate cash and lack access to traditional credit. The main caveat is that pawn loans are expensive relative to traditional loans due to short terms and high interest rates, and customers risk losing their pledged items entirely if they cannot repay.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal property (jewelry, electronics, tools, instruments, appliances)
Gold, silver, and platinum buying for immediate cash
Retail sales of used merchandise across multiple categories
Layaway service with 10% down payment option
Store locator tool to find nearest of 3,300+ locations
Pawn FAQs and educational content on how pawn loans work
Lease-to-own and retail finance solutions through AFF subsidiary (15,000+ merchant partners)
Point-of-sale payment solutions for credit-constrained consumers
Wide inventory of merchandise (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, instruments)
